title: Languages of the Oasis
synopsis: When a chubby baby emperor penguin finds himself at a desert oasis school, he must learn to communicate with a young camel who speaks a different language. Through gestures, pictures, and patient understanding, they discover that friendship needs no words.

pages.text:
Sand whispers secrets in the morning breeze,
At the oasis school beneath the date palm trees.
Waddles waddled up with his backpack tight,
But something here just didn't seem quite right.

The other students chattered, fast and free,
In sounds that puzzled him immensely.
"Marhaba! Ahlan!" they called with glee,
While Waddles chirped, "Hello!" quite helplessly.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet stands confused at a desert oasis school with date palms and a quiet spring. Other animal students are chattering in the background. Chalkboard style art with white and colored chalk on dark green board, school themed with dusty texture, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Waddles looking confused as other students speak in a different language at the oasis school

pages.text:
A young camel with kind, brown eyes
Noticed Waddles' look of surprise.
She spoke in words he couldn't understand,
Then pointed gently with her sandy hand.

"What did you say?" chirped Waddles small,
"I didn't understand at all!"
The camel tilted her long, graceful head,
And tried once more with words unsaid.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet faces a young camel at the desert oasis with date palms and a quiet spring. They look puzzled but kind toward each other. Chalkboard style art with white and colored chalk on dark green board, school themed with dusty texture, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Waddles and a young camel trying to communicate but not understanding each other's words

pages.text:
The sun climbed high, the shadows grew small,
At lunchtime in the date palm hall.
Waddles sat alone upon a stone,
Feeling very far from home.

The camel approached with lunch to share,
And gestured to the empty chair.
She pointed to herself: "Layla," she said,
Then pointed to him and tilted her head.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet sits on a stone at the desert oasis with date palms and a quiet spring during midday. A camel approaches with food to share. The sun is high overhead. Chalkboard style art with white and colored chalk on dark green board, school themed with dusty texture, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Waddles sitting alone while the camel Layla approaches with lunch to share

pages.text:
"Oh!" chirped Waddles, understanding dawned,
"I'm Waddles!" and he tried not to yawn.
Layla smiled and clapped her hooves with joy,
Names are bridges every friend employs!

She offered dates, so sweet and brown,
Waddles shared his fish, fresh from town.
No words were needed for this simple feast,
Kindness travels from west to east.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet shares lunch with a camel named Layla at the desert oasis with date palms and a quiet spring. They're exchanging food - dates and fish - with happy expressions. Chalkboard style art with white and colored chalk on dark green board, school themed with dusty texture, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Waddles and Layla sharing their different foods and smiling together

pages.text:
Art class came when the day grew warm,
Layla showed Waddles the desert art form.
She drew in sand with her careful toe,
A picture-story for him to know.

First a camel, then a penguin too,
Playing together in the afternoon hue.
Waddles clapped his flippers with delight,
"I understand! Your drawing's just right!"

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet watches as a camel draws pictures in the sand at the desert oasis with date palms and a quiet spring. The drawings show a camel and penguin playing together. Chalkboard style art with white and colored chalk on dark green board, school themed with dusty texture, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Layla drawing pictures in the sand to communicate with Waddles during art class

pages.text:
Then Waddles tried with his webbed feet wide,
To draw his home by the ocean side.
Snow and ice, and fish swimming free,
Layla watched most carefully.

"Wow!" she said, though he didn't know the word,
Her amazement was clearly heard.
Pictures speak what voices cannot,
Friends find ways, whether speaking or not.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet draws pictures of his icy home in the sand while a camel watches with wonder at the desert oasis with date palms and a quiet spring. The sand drawings show snow, ice, and fish. Chalkboard style art with white and colored chalk on dark green board, school themed with dusty texture, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Waddles drawing pictures of his snowy home while Layla watches with amazement

pages.text:
As evening painted the oasis gold,
A friendship story began to unfold.
Layla taught Waddles to count in her way,
"Wahid, ithnaan, thalatha!" through their play.

Waddles chirped back his numbers with pride,
"One, two, three!" as they walked side by side.
Laughter sounds the same in every tongue,
Songs of friendship are joyfully sung.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet walks alongside a camel in the golden evening light at the desert oasis with date palms and a quiet spring. They appear to be counting together and laughing. Chalkboard style art with white and colored chalk on dark green board, school themed with dusty texture, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Waddles and Layla walking together in the golden evening, teaching each other to count

pages.text:
The spring reflected the evening stars,
As they sat together, no barriers, no bars.
Layla pointed up to the crescent moon,
Waddles chirped a happy tune.

Different words, but the same warm heart,
This was just their friendship's start.
At the oasis school beneath the date palm trees,
Friends find ways with the greatest of ease.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet sits beside a camel by the quiet spring at the desert oasis with date palms under a starry evening sky with a crescent moon. They look content and peaceful together. Chalkboard style art with white and colored chalk on dark green board, school themed with dusty texture, hand-drawn on board.
illustration.alt: Waddles and Layla sitting peacefully together by the spring under the evening stars

coverPrompt.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet stands next to a young camel at a desert oasis with date palms. Both are drawing pictures in the sand. Chalkboard style art with white and colored chalk on dark green board, school themed with dusty texture.
coverPrompt.alt: Cover showing Waddles the penguin and a camel communicating through drawings at an oasis

questions.question: What did Layla do to help Waddles understand her?
questions.explanation: Layla drew pictures in the sand during art class to communicate with Waddles when words weren't working.

questions.question: Where does this story take place?
questions.explanation: The story happens at a school located at a desert oasis with date palm trees and a quiet spring.

questions.question: What did Waddles and Layla share at lunchtime?
questions.explanation: Layla offered dates and Waddles shared his fish, showing how they could be kind without using words.

questions.question: What did they teach each other in the evening?
choices: How to count in different languages
questions.explanation: In the evening, Layla taught Waddles to count in Arabic while Waddles taught her to count in English.

questions.question: How did Waddles feel by the end of the story?
choices: Happy and like he had found a friend
questions.explanation: By evening, Waddles had found a true friend in Layla, showing that friendship can grow even without shared language.
